issues.title: Daily Log page lacks loading, error, and empty states per acceptance criteria
issues.description: The Daily Log page does not show skeleton loaders matching final layout geometry on load, has no contextual empty state ('No daily logs yet — the daily log is your project's official record'), and no inline retry on network failure. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §7 items 11-12 are violated. Missing 'Today's daily log hasn't been started' persistent banner for superintendent+ when no log exists for today.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, make these specific changes: In the main DailyLog component, add three states. BEFORE: The component renders data directly or shows a generic spinner/blank page when loading, shows nothing meaningful when daily_logs query returns empty array, and does not handle fetch errors gracefully. AFTER: (1) When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader with 4 metric card placeholders (matching the 1x4 grid), a calendar placeholder, and 3 row placeholders matching daily log entry height, using pulse animation (opacity 0.3 to 0.7, 1.5s cycle). (2) When data is loaded but the array is empty, render an empty state with a Calendar icon (from lucide-react), the text 'No daily logs yet. The daily log is your project's official record — start documenting site conditions.', and a primary CTA button 'Start Today's Log' that navigates to the create form with today's date pre-filled. (3) When a fetch error occurs, render a toast notification with the error message and an inline 'Retry' button that calls refetch(). (4) Add a persistent banner at the top of the page when no log exists for today's date: 'Today's daily log hasn't been started' with a 'Start Log' button. Add aria-live='polite' to the banner region. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Field Capture page not camera-first and missing offline queue indicator
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §14 requires 'Capture button opens camera immediately' and offline queue showing 'X photos pending upload.' Current implementation either shows a form-first flow or lacks the camera-first UX entirely. Touch targets are not verified at 44x44px minimum. No offline banner or pending sync count is displayed.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, make these specific changes: In the FieldCapture component, restructure the primary CTA and add offline awareness. BEFORE: The page shows a form or generic upload UI without prioritizing camera capture, has no offline indicator, and no pending upload count. AFTER: (1) The primary action button labeled 'Capture' must be at least 64x64px (well above 44px minimum), prominently placed at bottom-center on mobile (position: fixed, bottom: 24px), styled with the primary orange (#F47820), and on click it opens the native camera via an input[type='file'][accept='image/*'][capture='environment'] element. (2) Add an offline detection hook (navigator.onLine or existing useOffline hook). When offline, show a yellow banner at top: 'You are offline — photos will sync when you reconnect' with aria-live='assertive'. (3) Track queued uploads in component state or from an offline queue store. Display 'X photos pending upload' badge near the capture button when count > 0. (4) Add an empty state when no field captures exist: Camera icon, text 'No field captures yet. Start documenting site conditions with photos.', and the Capture CTA button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety page missing ARIA attributes on incident severity indicators and form controls
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §11 requires color-coded incident severity (First Aid=yellow, Medical Treatment=orange, Lost Time=red, Fatality=black) with OSHA standards. These severity indicators rely solely on color with no aria-label or screen reader text. The 'Days Since Last Incident' counter has no aria-live region for dynamic updates. Form toggles for incident creation lack proper label associations. Global standards require all status changes announced via aria-live regions.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, make these specific changes: In the Safety component and any child severity badge/indicator components rendered inline. BEFORE: Incident severity badges use only background color to convey meaning (yellow/orange/red/black) with no text alternative. The 'Days Since Last Incident' counter updates without screen reader announcement. Form inputs may lack explicit htmlFor/id label associations. AFTER: (1) On every severity badge/dot, add aria-label describing the severity level, e.g., aria-label='Severity: First Aid' or aria-label='Severity: Lost Time'. Also add a visually hidden span with sr-only class containing the severity text for screen readers. (2) Wrap the 'Days Since Last Incident' counter in a div with role='status' and aria-live='polite' so changes are announced. (3) For the TRIR metric card and open corrective actions count, add aria-label on the card container describing the metric, e.g., aria-label='Total Recordable Incident Rate: 1.2'. (4) Ensure all form inputs in the incident creation form have matching id and htmlFor on their labels. Add aria-required='true' on required fields. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Daily Log missing legally defensible immutability — submitted logs can be edited without audit addendum
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §7 item 6 states: 'Approved logs are permanently locked. Amendments create a linked addendum, not an edit.' Currently there is no enforcement preventing edits to submitted/approved logs. The CONSTRUCTION_DOMAIN_KNOWLEDGE doc states daily logs are 'legally discoverable: used in claims, disputes, delay analysis, insurance proceedings.' Without immutability, the entire daily log feature fails its core legal purpose.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, make these specific changes: In the DailyLog component and any inline edit/save handlers. BEFORE: All daily log entries can be edited regardless of their status (draft/submitted/approved). There is no visual lock indicator and no addendum workflow. AFTER: (1) After fetching daily log data, check the status field. If status is 'submitted' or 'approved', disable all form inputs by setting disabled={true} on every input/textarea/select element in the form. (2) Show a lock icon (Lock from lucide-react) with a banner: 'This log was submitted on [date] and is locked for editing.' For approved logs: 'This log was approved on [date]. Changes require an addendum.' (3) For submitted logs, only users with role project_manager or higher (check via usePermissions or role from auth store) should see a 'Return to Draft' button that changes status back to draft with a required rejection note. (4) For approved logs, show an 'Add Addendum' button that opens a new form section appended below the original log. The addendum saves as a separate record linked to the original daily_log_id, with its own timestamp and author. The original log content remains unmodified. (5) Add a visual indicator on the daily log list/calendar view: a lock icon next to submitted/approved entries. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety page metric cards show placeholder values instead of real calculated KPIs
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §11 requires: Days Since Last Incident (resets on medical_treatment+ incidents), TRIR calculated as (Incidents x 200000) / Total Hours, Open Corrective Actions count, Inspections This Week count, and Certifications Expiring Soon. Current implementation shows hardcoded or zero values instead of computing from actual safety_incidents, daily_log manpower hours, and inspection data. The FOUNDER_PRIORITIES doc states: 'Every metric on every page should be a real calculated number, not a placeholder.'
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, make these specific changes: In the Safety component where metric cards are rendered. BEFORE: Metric cards display hardcoded numbers (e.g., '0' or placeholder values like '145' for days since incident) that do not derive from actual database queries. AFTER: (1) For 'Days Since Last Incident': query safety incidents where severity is 'medical_treatment', 'lost_time', or 'fatality', order by date descending, take the first result. Calculate days as Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(lastIncident.date).getTime()) / 86400000). If no such incident exists, show 'No recordable incidents' with a green indicator. (2) For TRIR: query total recordable incidents (severity >= medical_treatment) for the project and total crew hours from daily_log_entries. Calculate: (recordableCount * 200000) / totalHoursWorked. Display with 2 decimal places. If no hours data, show 'N/A — log crew hours to calculate'. (3) For Open Corrective Actions: count safety_incidents or corrective_action records where status is 'open'. (4) For Certifications Expiring Soon: count certifications where expiry_date is within 30 days of today. If the query returns mock data or the tables don't exist yet, use the existing mock data but format the numbers correctly and add a subtle '(sample data)' indicator. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Fieldwire dominates field operations with true offline photo capture, one-tap camera UX, and instant sync. Their daily log is completable in under 60 seconds. SiteSync's field operations module currently lacks camera-first UX, offline queue visibility, and the 90-second daily log benchmark defined in acceptance criteria. Procore's Quick Capture (video to punch item with auto-transcription) is a direct threat. SiteSync's safety module is behind both Procore and Fieldwire — neither competitor has AI PPE detection, but both have functional OSHA 300 log generation which SiteSync lacks.
summary: The Field Operations module (Daily Log, Field Capture, Safety) scores 40/100 — well below the 70 threshold for functional adequacy. The five most impactful issues are: (1) Daily Log lacks loading/empty/error states required by acceptance criteria, making the page feel broken on first visit; (2) Field Capture is not camera-first and has no offline queue indicator, violating the core 'field-first' product principle; (3) Safety page severity indicators have zero accessibility support, failing WCAG AA and making the page unusable for screen reader users; (4) Daily Log has no immutability enforcement on submitted/approved logs, undermining its legal defensibility — the single most important property of a construction daily log; (5) Safety metric cards show placeholder values instead of calculated KPIs, violating the founder's explicit priority that every metric must be a real number. All five issues are surgical fixes to existing components — no new features, no redesign, just making the existing code meet its defined acceptance criteria.
